heading: Hearings on denials of applications for naturalization
label: 1447 Hearings on denials of applications for naturalization
notes: Amendments1991—Subsecs. (d), (e). , (h), amended , (E)(ii), respectively. See 1990 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 102–232, § 305(g)Pub. (c) which prescribed a waiting period of thirty days after the filing of a petition for naturalization for the holding of a final hearing and permitted waiver of such period by the Attorney General if he determined that a waiver was in the public interest.Pub. L. 97–116, § 15(d)(2)Subsec. (d). , (4), redesignated subsec. (e) as (d) and struck out provision permitting the substitution of witnesses if after the petition is filed any of the verifying witnesses appear to be not competent, provided the petitioner acted in good faith in producing such witness. Former subsec. (d) redesignated (c).Pub. L. 97–116, § 15(3)Subsec. (e). , (5), redesignated subsec. (f) as (e). Former subsec. (e) redesignated (d).Pub. L. 97–116, § 15(d)(4)Subsec. (f). , redesignated subsec. (f) as (e).Pub. L. 97–116, § 15(d)(5)1969—Subsec. (c). struck out requirement that Attorney General, as a prerequisite to waiver of the waiting period, make an affirmative finding that such waiver will promote the security of the United States, and further struck out the provision prohibiting the acquisition of citizenship by final oath within 60 days preceding a general election and prior to the tenth day following such election.Pub. L. 91–136

children.heading: Request for hearing before immigration officer
children.label: a Request for hearing before immigration officer
children.text: If, after an examination under , an application for naturalization is denied, the applicant may request a hearing before an immigration officer.section 1446 of this title

children.heading: Request for hearing before district court
children.label: b Request for hearing before district court
children.text: If there is a failure to make a determination under before the end of the 120-day period after the date on which the examination is conducted under such section, the applicant may apply to the United States district court for the district in which the applicant resides for a hearing on the matter. Such court has jurisdiction over the matter and may either determine the matter or remand the matter, with appropriate instructions, to the Service to determine the matter.section 1446 of this title

children.heading: Appearance of Attorney General
children.label: c Appearance of Attorney General
children.text: The Attorney General shall have the right to appear before any immigration officer in any naturalization proceedings for the purpose of cross-examining the applicant and the witnesses produced in support of the application concerning any matter touching or in any way affecting the applicant’s right to admission to citizenship, and shall have the right to call witnesses, including the applicant, produce evidence, and be heard in opposition to, or in favor of the granting of any application in naturalization proceedings.

children.heading: Subpena of witnesses
children.label: d Subpena of witnesses
children.text: The immigration officer shall, if the applicant requests it at the time of filing the request for the hearing, issue a subpena for the witnesses named by such applicant to appear upon the day set for the hearing, but in case such witnesses cannot be produced upon the hearing other witnesses may be summoned upon notice to the Attorney General, in such manner and at such time as the Attorney General may by regulation prescribe. Such subpenas may be enforced in the same manner as subpenas under may be enforced.section 1446(b) of this title

children.heading: Change of name
children.label: e Change of name
children.text: It shall be lawful at the time and as a part of the administration by a court of the oath of allegiance under for the court, in its discretion, upon the bona fide prayer of the applicant included in an appropriate petition to the court, to make a decree changing the name of said person, and the certificate of naturalization shall be issued in accordance therewith.section 1448(a) of this title
